channel.title: Bartow, FL - Pages
channel.description: Bartow, FL - Get the latest alerts

item.title: Children's Programs
item.description: Explore Children's Programs offered at the Public Library.

item.title: Teen Programs
item.description: Explore teen programs offered at the Public Library.

item.title: Adult Programs
item.description: Explore adult programs offered by the Public Library.

item.title: Board Vacancies, Board Applications, Board Handbook
item.description: Find documents related to boards and committees.

item.title: Community Redevelopment Agency (CRA)
item.description: The Community Redevelopment Agency (CRA) was created by the City of Bartow in 1990 to eliminate blight and promote the redevelopment of the property within the CRA district.

item.title: Electric
item.description: Electric

item.title: Recruiting
item.description: The Bartow Police Department employs 40 plus Police Officers and 25 civilian support personnel. To maintain the highest level of efficiency, we incorporate state-of-the-art equipment with ongoing innovative in-house training programs.

item.title: Achievers on Display - A CRA Banner Project
item.description: The Community Redevelopment Agency Banner Project, established in 2023, honors African Americans who have significantly impacted the City of Bartow.

item.title: CRA Incentive & Grant Opportunities
item.description: The Community Redevelopment Agency (CRA) has initiated new assistance programs and numerous grant opportunities aimed at enhancing both commercial and residential properties within the CRA District area.

item.title: Understanding Your Bill
item.description: Confused about your bill? Visit this page for help!

item.title: Bartow Library
item.description: Search the card catalog, renew your items, or join us for an event at the library.

item.title: City Manager's Office
item.description: Mike Herr was hired as Bartow City Manager in January 2023. Herr, a long-time public servant, previously served as City Manager in Winter Haven and Plant City as well as Polk County Manager, among other high-level municipal positions.

item.title: Campaign Treasurer's Reports
item.description: Please contact the city clerk's office for copies of the campaign treasurer's reports.

item.title: Public Notices
item.description: Please be advised that representatives of the City of Bartow and the International Union of Painters and Allied Trades No. 78, representing the Bartow General Employees bargaining unit will meet on Monday, October 4, 2021 at 9:00 A.M. in the City Commission Chamber at Bartow City Hall, 450 N. Wilson Ave., Bartow, FL 33830.

item.title: Cemeteries
item.description: City of Bartow Cemeteries include Wildwood and Evergreen

item.title: Hurricane Preparedness
item.description: Hurricane season starts June 1 and ends November 30. Before hurricane season each year, make sure you and your family are prepared by making plans.

item.title: Emergency Management
item.description: The City's Fire Chief also is in charge of Emergency Management for Bartow. Get information and updates about extreme weather events.

item.title: Employment
item.description: Interested in working at the Fire Department? Visit this page to learn more.

item.title: Emergency Management
item.description: The City's Fire Chief also in in charge of Emergency Management during hurricane season and in the event of other natural and / or man-made disasters.

item.title: Current Notices
item.description: Current alerts, notices and information pertaining to the fire department.

item.title: Fire Prevention Week
item.description: October is when Bartow Fire Department joins forces with the nonprofit National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) to review fire safety tips for our residents.

item.title: Citywide Fire Hydrant Testing
item.description: The City of Bartow Fire Department conducts annual fire hydrant testing and maintenance program. This program is very important to allow the City to identify the quantity of water available for firefighting purposes and it serves to rid the City’s water distribution system of accumulations of sedimentation deposits over the past year.

item.title: Our History
item.description: Explore Bartow Fire Department history.

item.title: City of Bartow Burning Regulations
item.description: Learn about burning regulations in Bartow.

item.title: Past Chiefs
item.description: View the past chiefs of the Bartow Fire Department.

item.title: BFD Kids Zone
item.description: View fire safety information for kids.

item.title: Personnel
item.description: Find information related to Bartow Fire Department career personnel.

item.title: Recycling
item.description: View a list of materials accepted for recycling.

item.title: Canvassing Board
item.description: The Canvassing Board Members include City Manager Mike Herr, City Attorney Sean R. Parker and City Clerk Jacqueline Poole.

item.title: Annual Budgets
item.description: View Finance Department Annual Budgets.

item.title: Garbage Collection
item.description: Garbage means all putrescible animal or vegetable wastes resulting from the handling, preparation, cooking, and consumption of food, or otherwise.

item.title: Residential Refuse Collection
item.description: The Solid Waste Department (SWD) provides waste collection services to over 5,600 households in Bartow.

item.title: Bartow Solid Waste Rates
item.description: Pursuant to Florida law, each year the City publishes the Full Cost Accounting (based on Unit Costs) for Residential, Commerical, and Recycling services.

item.title: What if I can't pay my bill?
Construction Requirements for New Electric Service
